content:
## When Provisional Credit Applies
Use provisional credit only after the credit-card dispute intake shows every required condition is met: transaction_status_is_posted, notice_is_timely, transaction_not_already_disputed, resolution_request_is_valid, dispute_amount_is_valid, reason_allows_provisional_credit. Treat the conditions as cumulative; if any one is missing or contradicted, do not present provisional credit as available. A transaction that is still pending, already in an active dispute, outside the accepted notice path, unsupported by the requested resolution, or outside the valid amount rules should continue through intake without an automatic provisional-credit commitment.

## Dispute Reasons
The qualifying reason set is unauthorized_fraudulent_charge, duplicate_charge, incorrect_amount, goods_services_not_received, canceled_subscription_still_charging, refund_never_processed. When the customer describes the issue in plain language, map the description to the closest supported reason before discussing credit treatment. If the reason cannot be matched, ask a narrow follow-up question rather than broadening the policy.

## Quality-Dispute Exception
The excluded reason is goods_services_not_as_described. For this synthetic policy, accepted goods/services quality disputes can be filed but do not receive provisional credit automatically. This means an accepted filing and provisional credit are separate decisions; do not imply that every filed credit-card dispute receives temporary credit.

## Intake Coordination
Use "Credit Card Billing Error Intake" for the facts needed before applying this rule. For customer-ready timing language, see "Credit Dispute Timing Faq".
title: Credit Card Provisional Credit Policy
